segments.s551-g289: This is a prepositional phrase: از + noun phrase = 'from/at the...'؛ آواز گرزش = 'the sound/cry of his mace' where گرز (mace) takes the possessive suffix -ش 'his'. همی is the older continuous particle meaning 'continually/ever' and modifies the following verbal clause (which appears in the next segment). روز جنگ = 'the day of battle' (noun + genitive).
segments.s551-g290: بدرد is a present-tense, transitive verb here meaning 'causes pain/penetrates/tears into' (the subject is 'روز جنگ' from the previous segment). دل شیر and چرم پلنگ are object phrases ('the lion's heart' and 'the leopard's hide'); the conjunction و links them. Classical Persian often omits the ezāfe or shows possession by juxtaposition, as here.
segments.s551-g291: سرش سبز باد uses باد as an optative particle meaning 'may it be', with سرش ('his head') as the subject of the wish — a conventional blessing 'may his head be green' (i.e. may he prosper/live). The second clause دلش پر ز داد = 'his heart full of justice' uses پر + ز + noun ('full of ...') where ز = 'of/from' and داد = 'justice'.
segments.s551-g292: This is a negative optative clause: جهان ... مباد = 'may the world not be ...'. بی‌سر 'without a head/leader' and افسر او 'his helmet/crown' form the predicate complement meaning 'deprived of head and his crown'; مباد is the classical negative 'may it not be', so the speaker wishes the world never be without the hero's head/crown.
segments.s551-g105: چنین باشد is an optative formula ('may it be thus') with باشد used like 'may it be'; اندازه عام شهر is a genitival phrase 'the measure/extent (اندازه) of the public/city' where عام qualifies the public/collective sense of شهر. Word order places the optative verb before the noun-phrase for poetic emphasis.
segments.s551-g106: ترا is the 2nd-person clitic (ت) plus the object marker را, fronted for emphasis; جاودان is an adjective/predicative describing ترا. از خرد باد is a compact optative clause where باد is the optative particle 'may', and از خرد gives the cause/reason 'by/because of wisdom'; بهر means 'for' or 'for the sake of'.
segments.s551-g107: بترس is an imperative 'fear!' or 'beware'; از introduces the object of fear; بد modifies مردم ('evil/bad people'); بدنهان is a compound-like noun used poetically to mean those with bad (hidden) disposition—here functioning as a modifier of مردم or as a descriptive plural.
segments.s551-g108: که introduces a subordinate clause 'for/that/when'; بر means 'upon/toward' and takes بدنهان again as its object; تنگ گردد is 'becomes narrow/strained' with گردد (گشت) as a present subjunctive/optative-like third person 'becomes' (poetic usage); جهان is the subject or sentential topic 'the world'. The clause explains the reason for the warning.
segments.s551-g109: سخن هیچ مگشای is a prohibitive: سخن 'word/speech' + هیچ 'no/none' + مگشای 'do not open/disclose' (negative imperative). با introduces the person 'with/to'; رازدار is 'confidant'. The verb is negative imperative directed at the addressee.
segments.s551-g110: که introduces the explanatory clause 'for/that'; او را places 'he' in topical/emphatic position with را (object/topic marker); بود is the past/stative 'was/is' used here to state a fact about him; نیز 'also' strengthens the warning; انباز is an archaic/obscure adjective (here meaning treacherous/double‑faced); و یار links 'and companion/friend'—so the line says a confidant may nevertheless be treacherous though he appears a friend.
segments.s551-g111: سخن را marks 'speech' with the object/topic marker را; تو is the stressed second‑person subject 'you'; آگنده is an adjective 'filled/laden'; دانی here is not simple 'know' but 'consider/regard' (second‑person present), and همی is a poetic present‑tense particle adding continuity ('you always/regard').
segments.s551-g112: ز گیتی is a prepositional phrase 'from/of the world' (ز is the poetic 'from'); پراگنده is an adjective 'scattered/widespread'; خوانی is second‑person present of خواندن used in the sense 'you call/name' here (not only 'read'); همی again gives the habitual/continuous sense 'always'. The clause comments on how the addressee characterizes speech in relation to the world.
segments.s551-g233: کنون is an adverb 'now' placed up front for temporal framing. پادشاهی شاپور is the thing to be spoken about (the object phrase 'the kingship/kingdom of Shapur'), and گوی is the imperative 'say/speak' placed at the end (a common poetic word order).
segments.s551-g234: زبان برگشای is an imperative instruction: برگشای is an archaic/imparative-subjunctive form 'open/unseal' applied to 'tongue' (i.e. 'speak, unstop your tongue'). از governs the following phrase 'می‌و سور' ('of/from wine and feast') and here means 'concerning' or 'about'. The final گوی repeats the imperative 'speak' for emphasis; the verb again appears at the end as in classical poetic Persian.
segments.s551-g235: بران is an adverbial 'thereupon/for that reason' linking to what was said before. آفرین here functions as the present-stem/agentive notion 'the Creator' (a poetic use of the verb stem). کافرین is the direct object 'the unbelievers', and آفرید is the main past-tense verb 'created' placed at the end.
segments.s551-g236: This is a simple coordinated list of direct objects ('place and time and earth') followed by the past verb آفرید. The repeated و connects the three nouns; the verb appears at the clause end as the predicate.
segments.s551-g237: هم قبل 'آرام' is emphatic 'also/both' (= 'peace too'); ازویست is a fused construction 'is from him' (از وی + است) with the copula attached — a typical older/poetic form. The second half mirrors the first: هم کار ازوی has an omitted copula (inverted object + source) meaning 'the deed/work is from Him.'
segments.s551-g238: Same pattern as the previous segment: هم آغاز/انجام 'also/result' + ازویست 'is from him' (copula fused), then و linking to فرجام ازوی where the copula is again omitted (expected from earlier clause). The repetition emphasizes that both result and final end originate from Him.
segments.s551-g239: A coordinated list ('sky and time and earth') precedes the compound verbal phrase کرده است ('has done/made' — present perfect). Subject is implied (the same divine agent mentioned earlier); the compound (کرده است) marks the perfect aspect.
segments.s551-g240: کم و بیش is an idiom 'little and much' = 'more or less' / 'to some degree'. گیتی is the noun 'world', and برآورده است is a perfect/passive-like verbal phrase 'has brought forth / has caused to exist' with the copula است; overall meaning 'he has, more or less, brought the world into being.'
segments.s551-g121: خردمند (subject) + باید (predicate of necessity) here means ‘is needed/necessary’. جهاندار شاه is a compound nominal phrase 'world‑ruler, king' placed after باید as the beneficiary/experiencer (i.e. 'needed by the world‑ruler king'); word order is topical/poetic (noun — modal verb — beneficiary).
segments.s551-g122: کجا functions as a locutive 'where/wherever'. هرکسی را is marked by the object/topic marker را for emphasis ('anyone' / 'whoever'), and بود nimbly expresses existence ('is/was' in classical usage) governing the predicate نیک‌خواه ('a well‑wisher'). Together: 'wherever anyone is a well‑wisher.'
segments.s551-g123: کسی ('someone') followed by the archaic relative particle کو (‘who/that’) introduces a relative clause. بود is the copula 'was/is' linking to the adjectival predicates تیز و برترمنش ('sharp/quick‑witted and high‑minded/haughty'). The order is typical classical Persian: relative particle precedes the verb.
segments.s551-g124: بپیچد is a present/subjunctive/indicative 3rd‑person verb meaning 'turns aside' or 'shuns'. ز is the poetic preposition 'from', and پیغاره و سرزنش are the things turned away from ('admonition/message and reproach/blame'). The verb governs the following prepositional phrase.
segments.s551-g125: مبادا introduces a negative wish/avoidance clause ('may it not be' / 'lest'). که links to the subordinate verb گیرد, here used in the sense 'should take/occupy'. به نزد تو جای = 'the place/position at your side / in your presence' (به نزد = 'at/near', تو = 'you', جای = 'place/position').
segments.s551-g126: چنین ('such') modifies the following noun. مرد‌گر here functions as a description of that man (an older/poetic formation; syntactically the head noun of the clause). باشدت is a jussive/subjunctive form meaning 'may he be to you' (‑ت is a dative/clitic 'to you'), and رهنمای is the predicative noun 'guide/adviser' — together: 'may such a man be your guide' (here negated by مبادا in the previous segment).
segments.s551-g127: چو (conditional 'if/when') + خواهی ('you want') introduces the nominal complement clause headed by که. بستایدت is a jussive/subjunctive meaning 'that he praise you' (the final ـت is the object clitic 'you'), and پارسا is the person desired to do the praising ('the pious one' / a virtuous man). Overall: 'If you want that the pious one praise you...'.
segments.s551-g128: بنه is an imperative/poetic imperative meaning 'put (aside)' or 'set down' (an older verbal form); خشم و کین = 'anger and vengeance/hatred' are the objects to be set aside. چو/چون شوی پادشا is a temporal clause 'when/when you become king' (چون = 'when', شوی = 'you become', پادشا = 'king'), giving the time/context for the command.
segments.s551-g249: 'همه' is the plural subject 'everyone/all'. 'پاک بودند' is the past-tense predicate ('were pure'), with 'پاک' an adjective and 'بودند' the past plural of 'to be'. The conjunction 'و' links a second adjectival predicate 'پرهیزگار' ('pious/devout') without repeating the verb, so both adjectives apply to 'همه'.
segments.s551-g250: 'سخنهایشان' is the noun 'their words/speeches' (سخن + plural suffix ها + possessive شان). 'برگذشت' is a past verb meaning 'passed beyond / exceeded' (here 'surpassed'). The preposition 'از' with 'شمار' ('number/count') yields the idiom 'برگذشت از شمار' = 'went beyond counting' (i.e. too many to count). Note the verb is past and the phrase is literal: 'their words passed beyond number.'
segments.s551-g251: 'کنون' is the adverb 'now'. 'بر سخنها' means 'to/upon the words' (بر used as 'on/to' with the plural سخنها). 'فزایش' is a nominal root 'increase/addition' and with the light verb 'کنیم' ('let us do / we should do') forms the verbal idea 'let us add/increase'. The verb 'کنیم' is first-person plural and here has a hortative/optative sense ('let us...').
segments.s551-g252: 'جهان‌آفرین' is the compound noun 'World‑Creator / the Creator'. The object marker 'را' marks it as the definite object. 'ستایش' (praise) plus the same light verb 'کنیم' yields 'let us praise' (noun + انجام دادن construction). Word order puts the object before the verbal phrase; 'کنیم' again is hortative/first‑person plural.
segments.s551-g253: 'ستاییم' is first-person plural from ستودن, here hortative 'let us praise' or 'we praise' (poetic/hortative mood). 'تاج شهنشاه را' is the object 'the crown of the king of kings' with the object marker 'را'. Note the verb appears before the object in this poetic word order, but syntactically 'ستاییم' governs 'تاج شهنشاه را'.
segments.s551-g254: 'که' introduces a relative clause ('which/that'). 'تختش' = 'his throne' (تخت + possessive suffix ش). 'درفشان' is an adjective 'dazzling / shining' used as a predicative/completive element. 'کند' is the third-person singular present/past auxiliary 'makes/causes' and takes 'ماه را' ('the moon' + object marker 'را') as its object; the clause literally says 'his throne makes the moon shining' — i.e. 'whose throne makes the moon shine' or 'whose throne causes the moon to glitter.' The verb agrees with تخت (singular).
segments.s551-g255: 'خداوند' here functions as 'the lord/sovereign' (a royal epithet). The repeated preposition 'با' ('with/by') introduces instrumental phrases: 'با فر' (with farr/royal glory), 'با بخش' (with bounty/gifts), and then 'و داد' ('and justice') — listing the qualities/instruments accompanying the lord. 'فر' is the culturally specific 'royal glory (farr)'; 'بخش' here means 'gifts or generosity'; 'داد' is the abstract noun 'justice.'
segments.s551-g256: 'زمانه' is the subject 'the age / the world'. 'به فرمان او' is an agentive/instrumental phrase 'by his command' (به = 'by', فرمان = 'command', او = 'his/he'). 'گشت' is the past of 'گشتن' meaning 'became/turned', and 'شاد' is the predicate adjective 'happy/joyful'; together 'زمانه ... گشت شاد' = 'the age became joyful.'
segments.s551-g81: چنان دان is an imperative/address meaning 'consider thus' or 'know this'. که introduces a subordinate clause 'that...'. شاهی is a noun 'kingship' (here the topic/subject). ب + دان + پادشاست forms the predicate: ب as a prefixed element links to دان/padshāh‑st to express 'is/consists in that' — overall: 'that kingship is that (i.e., such a thing)'.
segments.s551-g82: که here continues the relative clause 'who/that'. دور فلک را is the definite object: دور modifies فلک in an older/poetic sense ('the circuit/round of the heavens' or 'the heavens themselves'); را is the definite object marker. ببخشید is past (3sg) 'bestowed/gave' and راست is an emphatic/adverbial 'truly/indeed' placed after the verb.
segments.s551-g83: زمانی is a temporal adverb 'at one time/for a while'. غم پادشاهی is a genitive phrase 'the sorrow/grief of kingship' with پادشاهی as possessor. برد is the past 3sg form of بردن 'took/seized' used idiomatically: 'grief seized (him/the king)'; the actual experiencer is implied from context.
segments.s551-g84: رد 'rejection' or 'refusal' begins the clause, followed by و 'and'. موبدش 'his mobed' (mōbed + possessive -ش) is the subject/agent. رای here means 'counsel/plan' and پیش آورد is a compound 'bring forth before' (پیش 'in the presence of' + آورد 'brought'), so 'the mobed brought his counsel forward (before the king)'.
segments.s551-g85: بپرسد is an archaic/narrative past 3sg form 'he asked' (prefixed ب- is the older verbal form). هم is an adverb 'also/too'. از introduces the object 'about/concerning' and کار بیداد و داد is the noun phrase 'the affair/matter of injustice (بیداد) and justice (داد)'.
segments.s551-g86: کند is the verb 'makes/does' (here narrative/past sense 'caused/made'). این سخن is the noun phrase 'this saying/utterance' as subject. بر دل شاه یاد is a prepositional phrase 'on the king's heart a memory/reminder' with یاد 'memory/reminder' as the resulting state: literally 'this saying made a memory on the king's heart' = 'impressed itself on the king's heart.'
segments.s551-g87: به روزی is a temporal phrase 'on a day' (به + روزی), with که introducing the relative clause 'that/when'. رای شکار is 'the plan/counsel of the hunt' (رای = plan/counsel, شکار = hunt), and آیدت is the 2nd-person enclitic verb 'comes to you' or 'befalls you' (آید + -ت).
segments.s551-g88: چو here functions as 'when'/'as', introducing a simile/temporal clause. یوز درنده is a noun phrase 'a fierce/rapacious cheetah' (یوز = cheetah, درنده = predatory/ferocious). به کار آیدت is an idiom 'be of use to you' (به marking beneficiary/benefactive + کار 'use'/'function' + آیدت 'comes to you'), meaning 'proves useful to you' or 'serves you'.
segments.s551-g273: سر نامه is an adverbial/locative phrase meaning “at the head (beginning) of the book”; it precedes the verb for emphasis. کردم is the first-person past tense “I did/made.” ثنای is a direct object meaning “praise,” and ورا is the dative/oblique pronoun “to him/of him,” attaching to the praise (classical Persian often uses ورا where modern Persian would useِ او or از او).
segments.s551-g274: This is a conjoined noun phrase listing qualities: بزرگی “greatness,” آیین “custom/practice,” رای “counsel/plan,” with the conjunction و (“and”) between them; the final ورا (dative) governs the whole series, so all three nouns are understood as ‘his X.’
segments.s551-g275: ازو is an oblique/topic pronoun meaning “of him/from him” and introduces the person the statement concerns. دیدم is simple past “I saw” (first person). اندر جهان is a locative phrase “in the world,” and نام نیک is the noun + adjective “a good name” (نام here = reputation/fame, with the adjective نیک following the noun as is common in classical Persian).
segments.s551-g276: ز here is the poetic ablative/causal “from/throughout,” and گیتی = world/earth. ورا is the dative “to him.” باد is the optative particle “may” used to express a wish, introducing the wished-for predicate فرجام نیک (“a good end/outcome”). Word order is slightly inverted: ‘from the world to him may (be) a good end.’
segments.s551-g277: ز دیدار او is a causal/ablative prepositional phrase “because of/from the sight/meeting of him.” دیدار is the noun “meeting/sight,” او the pronoun “he.” تاج روشن شدست is the result clause: تاج (crown) + روشن (bright) + شدست, an archaic perfect/auxiliary form meaning “has become” (شدست ~ شده‌است). The adjective روشن follows the noun as predicate.
segments.s551-g278: ز بدها is another causal phrase “from/because of the bad things/misfortunes.” ورا is the dative “to him.” بخت جوشن شدست is a nominative phrase where بخت (fortune/luck) is equated with جوشن (a cuirass/armor) by the verb شدست “has become,” i.e. ‘his fortune has become (like) armor.’ The structure uses noun + noun predicative followed by the archaic auxiliary شدست.
segments.s551-g279: بنازد is a classical/poetic verb from نازیدن meaning here ‘to take pride in / to be proud of’ and is used impersonally or as a jussive ‘may … be proud.’ بدو is the dative pronoun ‘of him/to him.’ مردم پارسا is the subject phrase ‘the pious people’; the verb preceding the subject is an instance of inverted classical word order for emphasis.
segments.s551-g280: هم‌انکس is a demonstrative ‘that very person’; که is the relative pronoun ‘who/that.’ شد is past ‘became,’ and the following prepositional phrase بر زمین پادشا places the result: ‘became king upon the earth.’ The relative clause identifies which person the pious people are proud of.
segments.s551-g241: This is a prepositional range: ز ... تا connects two endpoints ('from ... to ...'). ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. خاشاک ناچیز is a noun + adjective ('chaff, insignificant'), and عرش راست is the endpoint: عرش 'Throne' with راست as a qualifying adjective meaning 'right/true/lofty'. The overall phrase gives the spatial/metaphorical span.
segments.s551-g242: سراسر is an adverb 'throughout'. به here functions with گواست: 'witness to' (i.e. 'is witness to/over'). هستی is the noun 'existence' and یزدان is the subject 'God'. گواست is an archaic/poetic third-person singular verbal predicate meaning 'is witness' (verb placed at sentence end as usual in Persian).
segments.s551-g243: جز ... را ... is a prohibitive construction here: جز او را (except him) is the object marked by را, and مخوان is the negative imperative 'do not call/read' in literary Persian. کردگار جهان is in apposition to 'him' (i.e. 'the Maker of the world').
segments.s551-g244: شناسنده is a present-participle/agent-noun 'knower' that stands in apposition to کردگار جهان. آشکار و نهان are nouns/adjectives 'manifest and hidden' governed by شناسنده (i.e. 'knower of the manifest and the hidden'). The coordinating و simply links the two complementary terms.
segments.s551-g245: ازو is a clitic pronoun 'from Him' indicating source. بر + noun (بر روان محمد) is the prepositional phrase 'upon the soul of Muhammad'. درود is the object 'blessings/greetings' placed after that phrase; the whole is a clause meaning 'blessings upon Muhammad's soul from Him'.
segments.s551-g246: This clause contains an indirect-dative-like phrase and a verb: بیارانش here functions historically/poetically as 'to his companions' (written or printed with fusion), بر هریکی 'upon each one', and برفزود is the past tense, 3rd singular (literary) meaning 'he increased' or 'he bestowed/increased upon'. The subject (He/God) is omitted and supplied from context. Note: the input lemma 'بیارانش' is a fused form; its intended sense is 'to his companions'.
segments.s551-g247: سرانجمن is a compound noun 'head/leader of the assembly'. بد is an adjective 'bad/wicked' qualifying it. ز + یاران علی is a genitival/source phrase 'from/of the companions of Ali' (z here means 'among/from'). The whole is a nominal group 'a bad leader among Ali's companions.'
segments.s551-g248: که introduces a relative clause 'who/whom'. خوانند is the literary present third-person plural 'they call' (used here as a general reportive present). او را is the object 'him' with the object marker را, and علی ولی is the name plus title: علی (the proper name) followed by ولی used as the honorific 'Wali/saint'.
segments.s551-g57: This short noun phrase begins with the preposition به (’to/unto’) followed by three nouns joined by و (and). In Persian the preposition precedes the series: به بخشندگی یاز و دین و خرد = ‘turn to/lean on generosity, religion, and wisdom.’ یاز here functions as an imperative/hortative verb-like word meaning ‘turn to’ or ‘resort to’ and attaches syntactically to به as its verbal notion.
segments.s551-g58: دروغ ایچ تا با تو برنگذرد is an admonition clause. ایچ (an emphatic negative: ‘not at all’) + تا + negative verb برنگذرد (present/subjunctive form of برگَذَرد ‘pass by’) yields a purposive/optative sense: ‘so that not even a lie passes with you’ — in normal idiomatic English: ‘let no lie at all pass by you.’ با تو marks accompaniment/possession (‘with you’).
segments.s551-g59: This clause uses the normal Persian order where the possessor follows the possessed: رخ پادشا = ‘the king’s face/appearance.’ تیره دارد literally ‘has darkness’ (دارد = ‘has’/possesses) functions idiomatically as ‘becomes/appears dark.’ دروغ is the cause: ‘dark because of falsehood’ or ‘darkened by falsehood.’
segments.s551-g60: بلندیش is بلند + possessive suffix -یش (‘its height/loftiness’). هرگز نگیرد is a negated present/subjunctive/prophetic sense with هرگز ‘never’ + نگیرد (‘will not take/seize’). فروغ is the object ‘radiance/splendor.’ Together: ‘its loftiness will never take on radiance’ = ‘its loftiness never gains brightness.’
segments.s551-g61: نگر is an imperative (‘look!’ / ‘beware!’ / ‘consider’). تا نباشی gives a purpose/result clause ‘so that you are not’ (نباشی is the present/subjunctive form of بودن ‘to be’ with negation). نگهبان گنج is the predicate ‘guardian of the treasure.’ The whole phrase is an admonition: ‘Beware, so that you do not become the treasure’s guardian.’
segments.s551-g62: که introduces an explanatory/cause clause here (‘for/because’). مردم ز دینار یازد به رنج: ز + noun = ‘because of/for (the sake of) dinars’; یازد (poetic verb) ‘rush forth / break out / set about’ and the phrase به رنج ‘into hardship/to toil’ gives the result. So: ‘for people, because of dinars, rush into hardship.’
segments.s551-g63: اگر marks a conditional (‘if’). پادشا آز گنج آورد literally ‘if the king’s greed brings/causes treasure’ — آز is a noun ‘greed/avarice,’ گنج ‘treasure,’ and آورد here is used in the sense ‘cause/bring about’ (it can mean both literal bringing and ‘cause’ in poetic usage).
segments.s551-g64: تن زیردستان = ‘the bodies/persons of the subjects’ (تن ‘body/person’ with the plural noun زیردستان ‘subordinates/subjects’ modifying it). به رنج آورد repeats آورد in the sense ‘bring/cause to’ and به رنج ‘to hardship/suffering.’ Grammatically this is the consequence clause: ‘he brings his subjects’ bodies to suffering.’
segments.s551-g97: به فردا is a prepositional/time phrase 'to/till tomorrow'. ممان is an archaic negative imperative (m‑ + verb) meaning 'do not withhold/defer'; کار امروز را is the definite object (کار with the time‑modifier امروز, marked by را). Word order is topical/time phrase + negative imperative + object. The sense: 'Do not defer today's work to tomorrow.'
segments.s551-g98: بر تخت = 'upon the throne' (بر + تخت). منشان here is an archaic verbal form (m‑ + نشان, used imperatively) meaning 'do not seat / do not set (on)' in the same negative‑imperative pattern as ممان and مشنو; بدآموز را is the object 'the corrupter / ill‑instructor' with را. Together: 'Do not seat the corrupter on the throne.'
segments.s551-g99: مجوی is a negative imperative 'do not seek'. از دل عامیان is a prepositional/genitive phrase 'from the hearts of the common people' (دل = 'heart', عامیان = 'the common/public'). راستی is the object 'truth/uprightness' placed at the end for emphasis. The whole clause = 'Do not seek truth from common people's hearts.'
segments.s551-g100: که introduces an explanatory/casual clause here ('for / because'). از جست‌وجو 'by (or from) investigation/searching'; آیدت is the impersonal/affective present 'comes to you' (آید + enclitic ت), and کاستی is the result noun 'lack/deficiency'. Literally: 'for by searching a deficiency will come to you' = 'for your searching will bring you loss.'
segments.s551-g101: وزیشان is an older compound meaning 'from them' (وز = archaic/poetic 'from' + شان 'them'); ترا‌گر is a conditional address particle combination ('ترا' = to you, + 'گر' = if/should), so ترا‌گر بد آید خبر = 'if bad news comes to you (from them)'. بد آید is present/subjunctive 'comes ill / arrives badly' and خبر is the noun 'news'.
segments.s551-g102: تو مشنو is the negative imperative 'you (tu) do not hear / do not listen'. ز بدگوی = 'from the slanderer / ill‑speaker' (ز = poetic 'from'). و انده مخور is a parallel negative clause: مخور is negative imperative of خوردن 'do not eat', used idiomatically with انده (a poetic/archaic form related to اندوه 'sorrow') meaning 'do not be consumed by sorrow'. So: 'do not heed slanderers, and do not let sorrow eat you.'
segments.s551-g103: نه ... و نه is the correlative negation 'neither ... nor'. خسروپرست is 'one who worships/flatters the king' (a sycophant), and یزدان‌پرست is 'one who worships God / a pious devotee'; the line advises against becoming either extreme. Grammatically both nouns are coordinate objects of the negation.
segments.s551-g104: اگر is the conditional 'if'. پای گیری is a verbal nominal construction: پای (base/foot) + گیری (from گرفتن 'to seize/take'), here meaning 'if you seize the foot/base'. سر آید به دست = 'the head comes into (your) hand' (آید = 'comes', به دست = 'into hand/into your power'). This is proverbial/figurative: 'if you secure the foundation, the top (reward/control) will come into your hand.'
segments.s551-g113: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'. رازت is راز (secret) with the enclitic -ت 'your' (i.e. 'your secret'). به شهر آشکارا is a locative phrase 'in/to the city, openly' with آشکارا as an adverb following the locative; شود is 3rd-person singular 'becomes / is revealed'. The clause as a whole = 'when your secret becomes evident in the city'.
segments.s551-g114: دل is the noun 'heart' (here a collective idea). بخردان is the genitive/plural form 'of the wise' (the old/poetic order puts the possessed noun before its possessor), so دل بخردان = 'the hearts of the wise'. بی‌مدرا is a predicate adjective 'merciless' and شود makes it a change/state 'become merciless'.
segments.s551-g115: برآشوبی is the verb 'you disturb/you will stir up' (بر- prefix + آشوب root + 2nd‑singular ending -ی). و is the simple conjunction 'and/then'. سر سبک خواندت literally places the predicate 'light-headed' (سر سبک) before خواندت: خواندت is a past/transitive form with the object suffix -ت 'you' (i.e. 'they called you'), so 'they will call you light‑headed/frivolous.'
segments.s551-g116: خردمند‌گر is the fused conditional 'خردمند + گر' meaning 'if a wise (one)' (گر = 'if'). پیش here means 'before / in the presence of', and بنشاندت is a third‑person past/transitive stem بنشاند plus the object suffix -ت 'he seated you / he put you (in a place)'. The clause gives the conditional: 'if a wise man seats you before him'.
segments.s551-g117: تو is the explicit 'you' (address). عیب کسان uses the classical order noun + genitive (عیب 'fault' + کسان 'of people') = 'people's faults'. هیچ‌گونه is an adverbial 'in no way / at all' modifying مجوی, which is the negative imperative/present 'do not seek' (2nd‑singular).
segments.s551-g118: که here introduces the reason 'for/because'. عیب آورد is 'brings/carries a fault' (آورد = 'bring/cause'), بر تو is the prepositional phrase 'upon you / against you'. The final بر عیب‌جوی names the agent/recipient notionally related to that action ('by / from the fault‑finder' or 'the fault‑finder will bring it upon you'); overall: 'for the fault‑finder will bring (charge/find) a fault against you.'
segments.s551-g119: وگر is the conditional 'if'. چیره گردد is a predicative phrase 'becomes dominant / gains the upper hand' (چیره 'dominant/prevailing' + گردد 'becomes'). هوا is the subject 'desire/passion' and بر خرد is the prepositional 'over wisdom' (بر = 'over/upon', خرد = 'wisdom').
segments.s551-g120: خردمندت is 'your wise one' (خردمند + enclitic -ت 'your'). از مردمان is the prepositional phrase 'of/from the people' used with نشمرد to mean 'among the people'. نشمرد is negative imperative/command 'do not count / do not reckon / do not consider'. The clause = 'do not reckon your wise man among the people.'
segments.s551-g265: به رزم is a prepositional phrase meaning 'in/at battle' (به here = 'towards/into' used idiomatically as 'in'). آسمان را is the definite object marked by را ('the heavens/sky'), and خروشان کند is the predicate: خروشان ('roaring') + کند (light verb 'make/do') — literally 'makes the sky roaring' → 'makes the heavens roar.' Word order places the prepositional phrase and object before the verb, as is common in classical Persian poetry.
segments.s551-g266: چو introduces a temporal clause ('when'). بزم ('feast/banquet') is the occasion; آیدش is the verb with enclitic -ش referring to 'him' (literally 'the feast comes to him' but idiomatically 'when he comes to the feast'). گوهرافشان کند is the main clause: گوهرافشان ('gem‑scattering' / 'scattering gems') with کند as the light verb 'does/makes' → 'he scatters gems.' Note the subordinate temporal clause precedes the main clause.
segments.s551-g267: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when.' خشم آورد is a light/verbal phrase meaning 'brings forth anger' or 'his wrath rises' (آورد here = 'cause/bring about'). کوه ریزان is a nominal phrase 'mountain‑scattering' (کوه modified by ریزان), and شود ('become') yields a passive/result sense: 'mountains are made to scatter' or 'mountains crumble.'
segments.s551-g268: سپهر is the subject 'the sky/heavens.' از بر خاک is a prepositional complex 'from/upon the earth' (از = 'from', بر = 'on/upon', خاک = 'earth'). لرزان شود is 'becomes trembling' (لرزان = 'trembling', شود = 'become/be made'), so the clause means 'the heavens tremble above the earth' — a poetic image of cosmic upheaval.
segments.s551-g269: پدر بر پدر is a reduplicative/genetic phrase 'father upon father' (= generation after generation / ancestors). شهریارست is a predicate meaning 'is kingly' (an adjectival or evaluative statement about that lineage). و شاه links to the next clause ('and the king...'), so this segment states the hereditary kingship or royal quality of the line.
segments.s551-g270: بنازد here is an archaic/poetic verb from نازیدن; in this line it functions as 'takes pride in / honors / adorns' and is used with the indirect object بدو ('to him'). گنبد هور و ماه is the direct object 'the dome (crown) of sun and moon' — a laudatory image for a splendid crown. Word order is Verb + indirect object + direct object, typical of older Persian poetic usage.
segments.s551-g271: بماناد is an optative/benedictive verb form 'may (it) remain' (archaic wish form). تا جاودان is a temporal/completive phrase 'until eternal' → 'forever.' نام اوی is the possessed noun 'his name' (اوی = his). The order Verb + temporal phrase + object is typical for a wish in classical Persian.
segments.s551-g272: همه مهتری is a nominal phrase 'all preeminence/every superiority.' باد is the optative particle 'may' (classical Persian wish), and فرجام اوی is the complement 'his outcome/destiny' with اوی = 'his.' The structure is a benediction: 'May all preeminence be his fate.'
segments.s551-g185: The clause begins with که introducing an optative/wish: باشد is used in the sense of a subjunctive/optative 'may there be'. ز هر بد is a prepositional phrase 'from every evil' (ز = poetic 'from', هر = 'every', بد = 'evil'). نگهدارتان is a noun 'guardian' with the 2nd-person plural possessive suffix -تان, placed after the prepositional phrase (poetic inversion) so the whole means 'may (there) be your guardian from every evil'.
segments.s551-g186: This is a short optative clause: همه نیک‌نامی 'all good name/renown' is the subject/topic and بود is the copula used here in the sense 'be' (optative/hopeful). یارتان 'your companion/ally' has the possessive suffix -تان; the clause means 'may all good repute be your companion.' The word order (topic before verb) is typical of poetic diction.
segments.s551-g187: ز یزدان و از ما 'from God and from us' gives the sources of the blessing; بر آن کس درود is a fixed formula meaning 'greetings/blessings upon that person' (بر = 'upon', آن کس = 'that person', درود = 'greeting/blessing'). The clause is a benediction: 'blessings from God and from us be upon that person.'
segments.s551-g188: که introduces a relative clause describing 'that person.' تارش 'his/her tar' (a lute) is the possessed noun + suffix; خرد is used as an abstract noun 'wisdom' and باشد is the copula in the subjunctive/optative sense 'be.' داد پود pairs two nouns as attributes: داد 'justice' and پود 'weft/fabric' — literally 'whose tar is wisdom and whose weft is justice,' a poetic way to praise someone's art and moral fabric.
segments.s551-g189: شکست (object/topic) appears before the verb: نیارد here functions as a negative optative/preterite-like verb 'may not come/afflict' (poetic/inversional word order). اندرین عهد من = 'in this covenant/pledge of mine' (اندرین = 'in this', عهد = 'covenant', من = 'my'). The clause expresses a wish that this pledge suffer no defeat.
segments.s551-g190: نکوشد is a negative jussive/optative 'may he/one not attempt' (ن + کوشد). که introduces the subordinate clause describing the forbidden action: حنظل کند شهد من = 'that he make my honey (شهد من) into colocynth (حنظل)' where کند is the present-subjunctive of کردن 'make/do.' This line is a metaphorical warning: do not try to turn my sweetness into bitterness.
segments.s551-g191: برآمد is a past/perfective verb 'arose/elapsed/passed' and takes the time expression that follows: چهل سال 'forty years'. و بر سر دو ماه adds 'and two months more' (بر سر = 'on the head/over', here idiomatically 'in addition'), so the phrase means 'forty years and two months passed.'
segments.s551-g192: که تا introduces a temporal/purpose clause 'until' (که تا = 'so that / until'). برنهادم is the 1st-person singular past/subjunctive form of برنهاد 'to place on' rendered here as 'I placed / I should place on.' به شاهی کلاه = 'the crown (کلاه) of kingship (شاهی)' where به + شاهی marks the goal/office. Together: 'until I placed the crown of kingship (upon my head).'
segments.s551-g193: به گیتی is a locative prepositional phrase 'in the world' (به + گیتی). مرا here expresses possession in an older idiom ('for me' / 'I have'), followed by شارستانست (poetic fused form of 'شارستان است') meaning 'there are towns/cities'; شش is the numeral 'six' placed after the predicate. The whole chunk therefore says 'in the world there are six towns for me' (i.e. 'I have six towns in the world').
segments.s551-g194: هوا خوشگوار is a verbless nominal clause common in Persian: subject 'the air' + adjective 'pleasant' (the copula 'است' is omitted). و links the next clause. به زیر آب is another locative phrase 'under/beneath the water' and خوش is the adjective 'pleasant' predicated of that location. So: 'the air is pleasant, and under the water (also) pleasant.'
segments.s551-g195: یکی خواندم is 'once / I once read/heard' (یکی = 'a certain / once', خواندم = 'I read/recited / I heard of' in classical usage). خوره اردشیر is a proper name or title reported by the narrator; here it functions as the object of خواندم ('I read/heard of Khoreh Ardashir').
segments.s551-g196: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. گردد is the present/subjunctive/habitual form of 'گشتن' meaning 'become' (here narrative/gnomic use: 'becomes'). زبادش is a classical genitive/causal phrase 'from his abundance / by his great [something]' (ز = 'from', باد ~ 'abundance/excess' here), modifying the manner. جوان مرد is a compound meaning 'brave/youthful man' (جوان as 'vigorous/young' + مرد 'man/hero'), and پیر follows as an adjective 'old'; together the clause describes his transformation/state: 'who, through much of it, becomes a brave man, old.' Grammatically the adjectives/nouns stack to produce the idea 'a brave (yet) old man.'
segments.s551-g197: کزو is a relative/locative 'from which / from whom' linking to what came before. تازه شد is 'became renewed/fresh' (تازه + شد 'became'). کشور خوزیان is a genitival noun phrase 'the country/realm of the Khuzis' (کشور + خوزیان). The clause means 'from that (source) the realm of the Khuzis was renewed.'
segments.s551-g198: پر از ... is an idiomatic construction 'full of X' (پر + از). A series of nouns follows as items filling the country: مردم 'people', آب 'water', سود 'benefit/profit' and زیان 'loss/detriment'. The coordination lists what the land became full of.
segments.s551-g199: دگر here means 'another' / 'other'. شارستان is 'town/city' and گندشاپور is the proper name 'Gundeshapur'; نام following places emphasis on the name ('named Gundeshapur'). Word order is noun + name + نام (a poetic ordering meaning 'the city called ...').
segments.s551-g200: که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'. موبد is the subject 'a mobed' (Zoroastrian priest). ازان شهر is a locative/genitive phrase 'from that city' (ازان = 'from that/of that', شهر = 'city'). شد is the past 'became' and شادکام is the predicate adjective 'fortunate/joyful'. So: 'which: a mobed from that city became fortunate.'
segments.s551-g89: دو بازی (literally "two games/pastimes") is the direct object; بهم در زدن is a verbal phrase meaning "to do/perform together". نباید is the impersonal/modal negative "one must not/should not" and governs the following infinitive زدن (here "to do/engage in"). Word order is topical: the thing not to be done (دو بازی) comes first, then the negated verb phrase.
segments.s551-g90: This is a coordinated list of pastimes: می‌و بزم و نخچیر و بیرون شدن = "wine and feast, and hunting (prey), and going out" where several nouns/nominal infinitives are joined by و. شدن after بیرون makes the compound "to become out" i.e. "to go out/outing"; the whole phrase names the activities referred to by دو بازی above.
segments.s551-g91: که introduces a subordinate clause meaning "that/for". تن is the subject "the body/person", گردد (گردیدن) is a present-tense/optative form "becomes". از جستن is a prepositional phrase "from seeking/hunting", and می‌گران functions as a verbal adjective/participial sense "afflicting/causing weariness" modifying the result ("becomes afflicted/wearied by seeking").
segments.s551-g92: نگه داشتند is a compound verb meaning "they kept/maintained" (نگه = keeping/watch + داشتند = had/kept); این سخن is the direct object "this saying/words" and مهتران is the agent "the elders/chiefs" (an honorific plural). The verb is past tense (third person plural).
segments.s551-g93: وگر = "if/but if" begins a conditional. دشمن آید = "an enemy comes/arrives"; به جایی = "to/some place/anywhere" (locative), and پدید (پدیدار) "appears/manifests" reinforces the emergence. The clause overall: "if an enemy appears somewhere."
segments.s551-g94: ازین کارها = "from these affairs/matters" (ازین = 'from these'); دل بریدن is an idiom meaning "to cut off one's heart" i.e. "to abandon/cease caring about"; بباید (modal "one must/ought") + برید (here the infinitival/past-stem of بریدن used idiomatically) yields "one must break off/abandon."
segments.s551-g95: درم دادن and تیغ پیراستن are two coordinated infinitival actions: درم دادن = "to give/pay coin" (درم = dirham/coin + دادن = to give) and تیغ پیراستن = "to array/adorn the sword" (پیراستن = to array/arm). They form part of a list of actions.
segments.s551-g96: ز هر پادشاهی is a prepositional phrase "from every king/kingdom" (ز = from, هر = every, پادشاهی = realm/kingship). سپه خواستن is an infinitival phrase "to ask/request an army" (سپه = army, خواستن = to ask/request), completing the coordinated list begun in the previous segment.
segments.s551-g65: کجا (kajā) here functions as 'wherever/where' introducing a location; the poetic order places predicate before subject: نگهبان/بود patterns are common. بود is the past copula 'was', and the clause گنج اوست (treasure is his) uses ست (poetic contraction of است) as the present copula following the noun phrase 'گنج' with او as the possessor.
segments.s551-g66: وگر begins a concessive conditional here ('even if' / 'but if'). چند functions as 'how much / however little' qualifying the following phrase. بر + کوشش و رنج = 'on/over effort and toil' (بر marks comparison/measure), and ست is again the copula 'is', so the whole segment qualifies the preceding claim by conceding that the treasure may be small relative to the farmer's labour.
segments.s551-g67: This is inverted poetic word order: نگهبان بود شاه = 'the king was guardian' with بود as the past copula. گنج ورا attaches as the object/target ('of the treasure' / 'for it') — ورا is an enclitic referring back to the treasure or person affected.
segments.s551-g68: به بار آورد is a light-verb construction: به + بار (a load/burden) + آورد (brought/caused) = 'he caused/laid a burden'. شاخ رنج is a compound image 'branch (or great piece) of hardship'; the final ورا marks the recipient/target ('upon him/it'). The verb آورد is past tense 'brought/caused'.
segments.s551-g69: بدان کوش is an idiomatic sequence: بدən (be + an, 'at that/for that') + کوش ('strive'). تا introduces purpose ('so that'), دور باشی is a subjunctive/purposive clause 'you may be distant/keep away', and ز is the poetic variant of از meaning 'from' — ز خشم = 'from anger'. باشی is the present/subjunctive form 'you be / remain'.
segments.s551-g70: به مردی here means 'for manliness' / 'to be a man' (به + abstract noun مردی = 'for manhood'). به خواب = 'in sleep' or 'even in sleep', and از introduces the object avoided: گنهکار چشم = 'the eye/gaze from the sinner' — the implied verb is 'turn/keep (your) eye away'. The clause is prescriptive: a man should avert his gaze from the sinner, even in sleep.
segments.s551-g71: چو = 'when/if' introduces the condition. خشم آوری is a verb phrase ('you cause/bring about anger'); آوری is from آورد meaning 'bring about/produce' here. هم 'also/then' links result to prior thought, and پشیمان شوی is the result clause with شوید/شوی as the present/subjunctive 'you become' so the meaning is 'you will be/you become regretful.'
segments.s551-g72: به پوزش expresses means/instrument ('by apology' / 'through a pardon'). نگهبان is the agent/authority and درمان here is a noun 'remedy' or 'restoration'; شوی (become) makes the clause passive/benefactive in sense: 'you will be remedied/forgiven by the guardian through apology.' Word order puts the means first (به پوزش) then the agent and result.
segments.s551-g169: بپیچد is a 3rd-person singular subjunctive/jussive form (the b- prefix with present stem) meaning 'let/shall he turn' or 'may he turn'. Here it begins the clause and governs the idiom سر از عهد پیچیدن, literally 'to turn the head from the pledge', i.e. 'to break an oath' or 'to turn away from a covenant'. فرزند تو (your child/descendant) is the (understood) subject performing the action; Persian often places the verb before the explicit subject in poetic prose.
segments.s551-g170: هم‌انکس is a demonstrative/relativizing noun meaning 'that very person' or 'the same one'. که introduces a relative clause 'who is…'. باشد here is the present subjunctive/optative of بودن used as a simple 'is' ('who is/that is'), and ز پیوند تو literally 'of your bond/line' means 'of your kin' or 'from your family'. The whole phrase apposes/clarifies 'فرزند تو' as 'that very one who is of your line.'
segments.s551-g171: ز رای و ز دانش is a pair of prepositional phrases 'from counsel and from knowledge/learning'. به یکسو is an adverbial phrase 'to one side' or 'aside/one-sidedly'. شوند is 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive 'they become/turn', so the clause means 'they turn aside from counsel and learning' (i.e., abandon advice and learning). Word order places the prepositional objects before the verb in this poetic register.
segments.s551-g172: همان is emphatic 'that very' or 'the same'. پند means 'advice' or 'admonition'. دانندگان is the plural agent noun 'those who know' = 'the wise/experts'. نشنوند is the negative 3rd-person plural present (n- prefix + شنوند) meaning 'they do not hear/listen to' or 'they will not heed'. Together: 'they do not heed the very advice of the wise.'
segments.s551-g173: بگردند is 3rd-person plural present/subjunctive of گشتن (here 'turn/ become/act'), used to mean 'they turn' or 'they become'. یکسر is an adverb 'entirely/utterly'. ز عهد و وفا 'from oath and fidelity' indicates what they forsake. The clause therefore says 'they wholly depart from oath and loyalty.'
segments.s551-g174: به بیداد یازند uses به + noun + verb یازند (3rd-pl) where یازیدن here means 'break forth into' or 'turn to' (i.e. take up). So 'they take to outrage/injustice.' The following و جور و جفا lists related evils ('and injustice and cruelty'). The coordination is asyndetic/poetic: they abandon fidelity and instead fall into oppression and cruelty.
segments.s551-g175: جهان تنگ دارند is an idiomatic expression: تنگ داشتن (to make narrow) with جهان as its object means 'to make life/world narrow' i.e. to oppress or make life hard. دارند is 3rd-person plural 'they have/make'. بر زیردست 'for/against the underlings/subordinates' marks the people suffering. In sum: 'They oppress and make the world hard for their subordinates.'
segments.s551-g176: بر ایشان functions as a dative/target-marking prepositional phrase 'for/to them' (i.e. in their regard). شود is 3rd-singular present 'becomes' and خوار is an adjective 'despised'. یزدان‌پرست is the subject 'the God-worshipper/the pious'. The word order (prepositional phrase — verb — adjective after subject) yields the meaning 'the pious become despised in their eyes' or 'to them the pious are held in contempt.'
segments.s551-g137: مکن is the classical negative imperative 'do not'. خوار here is used predicatively/verb‑like ('treat as low/despised') so the clause means 'do not make (someone) خوار'. خواهنده درویش is the direct object ('the petitioner/poor dervish'), and را is the definite/direct‑object marker attached after the noun phrase.
segments.s551-g138: بر تخت = 'upon the throne' (بر + تخت). منشان is an archaic/poetic form related to نشان(دن) ('seat, set in place') and here functions with the preceding negative مکن to give the prohibition 'do not seat'. بداندیش را is the object 'the ill‑wisher' marked by را. In short: 'do not place the malicious one on the throne.'
segments.s551-g139: هرانکس که introduces a relative/subordinate clause 'whoever who…'. پوزش کند is 'makes an excuse/seeks pardon' (پوزش + the verbal auxiliary کند), and بر گناه specifies the object 'for the sin'. The verb کند is present/subjunctive style used to report the action of that indefinite subject.
segments.s551-g140: تو is the explicit/emphatic second‑person subject 'you'. بپذیر is the imperative 'accept' (take the pardon). و is the coordinating 'and'. کین گذشته = 'the past grudge/vengeance' (کین qualified by گذشته). مخواه is the negative imperative 'do not desire/ask for' — so: 'accept and do not seek vengeance for the past.'
segments.s551-g141: همه داده ده باش is an instruction: همه (to all/everyone) + داده‑ده ('giver' or 'giver‑of‑gifts') and باش is the imperative/subjunctive 'be'. The clause therefore means 'be a giver to all'. و پروردگار begins the next appositional phrase ('and (be) lord/sovereign').
segments.s551-g142: This is a string of descriptive modifiers applied to the ruler: خنک مرد = 'a calm/cool‑tempered man' (خنک modifies مرد), followed by two coordinating adjectival qualities بخشنده و بردبار 'generous and forbearing/patient'. There is no copula expressed; the adjectives qualify the implied 'be' from the previous clause.
segments.s551-g143: چو is the temporal/conditional 'when/if'. دشمن is the subject 'the enemy'. بترسد is a present/subjunctive form 'is frightened' or 'should fear', and شود ('become') here pairs with the predicate چاپلوس to mean 'becomes flattering/obsequious' or 'turns servile' — i.e. the enemy, once frightened, will behave obsequiously.
segments.s551-g144: تو is the emphatic 'you'. لشکر بیارای is an imperative clause 'array/prepare your army' (لشکر = army; بیارای here is a poetic/imperative form of arousing/arraying the troops). و coordinates the next command. بربند کوس is another imperative 'beat/sound the drum' (کوس = war‑drum; بربند functions as a command to strike/put up the drum signal).
segments.s551-g153: چو is a conditional 'if/when' introducing a protasis. بخشنده is an adjective 'generous' describing the subject (you). باشی is the present/subjunctive 2nd‑person singular form 'you be' used here in the protasis. گرامی شوی is the apodosis: گرامی (an adjective 'honored') + شوی (become, 2nd‑person singular), so literally 'you become honored.' The clause order is protasis → copular/result clause, typical of Persian poetic style.
segments.s551-g154: ز introduces the reason or source 'because of / from' in poetic register. دانایی is an abstract noun 'wisdom.' و links two causes ('and'). داد here means the moral quality 'justice' (not a verb). نامی شوی is parallel to the previous clause: نامی 'famous' + شوی 'you become' (2nd‑person singular), so 'you become famous/notable.'
segments.s551-g155: تو is the 2nd‑person singular pronoun 'you.' عهد پدر is the noun phrase 'the father's pledge/covenant.' با introduces the accompanying notion 'with / by,' and روانت is 'your soul/heart' (روان used for spirit/inner self). بدار here is an imperative/subjunctive form meaning 'keep/hold' (classical poetic command: نگهدار), so the clause literally 'You, keep your father's covenant with your soul.'
segments.s551-g156: به is the preposition 'to/for.' فرزندمان is 'our child/son.' هم‌چنین here means 'in the same way / likewise.' یادگار is a noun 'memento/keepsake.' The noun phrase orders are canonical: preposition + indirect object + modifier + noun — 'for our child, likewise a memento.'
segments.s551-g157: چو here functions as a temporal 'when.' من is the first‑person pronoun 'I.' حق is 'right/entitlement' (the rightful share). فرزند is 'child.' بگزاردم is a past first‑person form 'I caused/gave/entrusted' (ب‑ prefix + گذاشتم type meaning 'I gave/put in place'), so the clause reads 'When I gave/assigned the child's right.'
segments.s551-g158: کسی را is the object/topic 'anyone/someone' marked by the object marker را. ز گیتی is a poetic genitive/locative 'from/of the world' (z = from/of, گیتی = the world). نیازاردم is a classical/poetic past form expressing I did not harm/wrong/upset (here used negatively in sense 'I did not wrong anyone'); grammatically it is a 1st‑person past verb. Together the clause means 'I harmed no one of the world.'
segments.s551-g159: شما هم 'you also' (plural or polite). ازین عهد من combines ازین ('this') + عهد من 'my covenant/pledge' — از here marks 'from/of' or 'regarding' this covenant. مگذرید is the negative injunction 'do not let pass / do not ignore' (2nd‑person plural present/subjunctive/imperative form), so the clause means 'You too, do not overlook this my covenant.'
segments.s551-g160: نفس here means 'the essence/heart' (the inner self/essence) of something; داستان 'the story/tale' — نفس داستان را is therefore 'the very gist of the tale' with را marking the object. به is the preposition 'in/into/as' combined with بد 'badly/ill' to form به بد 'in a bad way.' مشمرید is an archaic/poetic 2nd‑person plural verb meaning 'you take/consider' (used in the negative sense with the previous clause), so the whole clause advises 'do not take the heart of the tale badly.'
segments.s551-g257: This is a nominal, appositive list introduced by خداوند (here an honorific meaning 'the sovereign'). The nouns گوپال، شمشیر، گنج are listed without a linking verb, a common poetic device: the speaker attributes these things to the sovereign (i.e. 'lord of/associated with X, Y, Z'). Word order is simple juxtaposition; و simply coordinates the list items.
segments.s551-g258: Again خداوند appears as the head noun and is followed by a coordinated list (آسانی و درد و رنج). No finite verb is present; the phrase attributes both comfortable and painful conditions to the same sovereign. و functions as the regular coordinating conjunction between nouns.
segments.s551-g259: جهاندار ('world-ruler/sovereign') is the main noun; با + فر ('with farr') uses با as an instrumental/possessive particle meaning 'possessed of' or 'with' (i.e. 'endowed with royal glory'). The following و + نیکی‌شناس coordinates a second epithet ('and a discerner/knower of good'). These are appositive descriptive phrases modifying the ruler.
segments.s551-g260: که introduces a relative clause ('who/that'). از تاج دارد is literal 'has/of the crown' — از + noun here indicates 'with respect to the crown' or 'for the crown he has'; دارد is the present-tense verb 'has/possesses' (here functioning as 'who has/holds'). به یزدان سپاس is a dative phrase 'thanks to God' or 'gives thanks to God' (سپاس used as a noun sometimes elliptical for 'offers thanks'). The whole clause reads: 'who, for the crown he has, gives thanks to God.'
segments.s551-g261: A string of coordinated adjectival epithets (خردمند و زیبا و چیره‌سخن) describing the same person. The adjectives are simply linked by و; they function attributively and are presented in balanced, poetic parallelism.
segments.s551-g262: جوانی بسال uses جوانی ('youth/young age') plus بسال ('in years/of years' — an archaic locution meaning 'in respect of years'), i.e. 'young in years'. و then links to بدانش کهن: بدانش ('with that knowledge' / 'in knowledge' — here meaning 'in terms of learning') + کهن ('ancient/old') gives the paradoxical phrase 'young in years and old in knowledge' (i.e. youthful in age but learned/ancient in wisdom).
segments.s551-g263: همی is a classical continuous/iterative marker ('ever, continually'). مشتری here is the planet Jupiter (classical astrological reference). بارد in this poetic context functions as a verbal form meaning 'falls/pours' (an archaic/poetic 3rd-person action); the surrounding structure is 'Jupiter continually pours from his cloud.' از introduces the source ('from the cloud') and اوی is a possessive/enclitic ('his').
segments.s551-g264: بتازیم is a classical present-form of the root تاز (to rush/charge); here it expresses vigorous, habitual action ('we/they rush' or more generally 'one/they rushes') — epic Persian often uses such plural/habitual forms impersonally. در سایه فر اوی is a prepositional phrase 'in the shadow of his farr' (در = 'in', سایه = 'shade/shadow/protection', فر = 'royal glory', اوی = 'his').
segments.s551-g145: به جنگ آنگهی شو is an instruction with the preposition به + noun (جنگ) meaning 'into/onto battle' followed by the imperative/hortative شو 'become/take up' (archaic/poetic imperative). آنگهی 'then/at that time' limits the timing. که introduces the result/purpose clause ('so that/when') and is followed by the subject دشمن and the preposition ز 'from/because of' governing جنگ ('from the fight' = 'avoiding battle').
segments.s551-g146: بپرهیزد is a third-person present/subjunctive form meaning 'he will avoid / he refrain(s)'; here it is the verb of the result clause introduced by که. و connects the two results. سست گردد is a copular/resultive expression: سست 'weak' + گردد 'become' (classical present subjunctive/optative 'become'), meaning 'become weak.' به ننگ uses به + ننگ to indicate direction/state ('into/with shame' = 'to shame').
segments.s551-g147: وگر is the conditional particle 'if.' آشتی جوید is verb + object: آشتی 'reconciliation/truce' and جوید (classical present/subjunctive of جوئیـدن) 'seeks/asks for'; the verb is in the same clause. و راستی links a second item sought/considered ('and [you see] truth/sincerity')—راستی here denotes sincerity/honesty rather than abstract philosophic truth.
segments.s551-g148: نبینی is a negative present/subjunctive 2nd-person form 'you do not see' used in the protasis ('if ... and you do not see'). به دلش uses به + دل + enclitic ش 'in/into his heart' (دلش = 'his heart'). اندرون کاستی places the deficiency inside: اندرون 'inside' and کاستی 'deficiency/shortcoming' (predicate noun). Word order is typical Persian: verb (نبینی) precedes the locative phrase that is the semantic object/predicate.
segments.s551-g149: ازو 'from him/of him' is the prepositional source. باژ بستان is an imperative phrase: بستان here functions as an imperative 'take' governing باژ (an item to be taken); however the provided sense list does not supply the verbal sense, so the exact lexical senses are uncertain. و links the next imperative clause; کینه مجوی is a negative imperative 'do not seek a grudge/hatred' (مجوی = 'do not seek').
segments.s551-g150: چنین دار is an instruction 'keep/hold thus' (چنین 'thus/so'). دار here functions as an imperative or verbal form meaning 'keep/retain' (the supplied noun senses do not match this verbal sense). نزدیک او 'near him' (نزدیک = 'near', او = 'him') specifies the position, and آب‌روی means 'reputation/face' — so the whole line directs keeping his reputation safe/near by acting as above.
segments.s551-g151: بیارای دل را is an imperative clause meaning 'adorn/beautify the heart' with را marking دل as a definite object. بیارای is an archaic/poetic imperative 'make beautiful/adorn' (the provided senses list lacks this verbal sense). به دانش 'with/to knowledge' marks the means/instrument (here 'by learning'). که ارز begins a clause 'for/value...' explaining the reason (که = 'for/that').
segments.s551-g152: به دانش بود is a verbless/copular idea rendered with بود 'is/was' — 'value lies in knowledge' (بود here states existence/attribution). تا توانی بورز is a purposive/limit clause: تا + second-person ability توانی ('as far as you can') followed by بورز (imperative/subjunctive 'practice/cultivate'), meaning 'exercise it to the extent you are able.'
segments.s551-g17: شما را: را here marks the person as the dative/experiencer (literary Persian often uses را after pronouns to mark the one affected). همان رنج: a noun phrase 'that same suffering'. پیشست: poetic contraction of پیش است ('is before'); the verb/copula follows the noun phrase and says the suffering 'is before you'. The conjunction و simply links the following noun 'ناز' (the verb is omitted by ellipsis, so 'and (there is) indulgence').
segments.s551-g18: زمانی ... زمانی: a repeated adverb 'at times' (here 'for a while/occasionally') modifying the two nouns. نشیب و فراز: paired nouns 'descent/low point and ascent/height'; the verb is omitted (elliptical) and understood from the previous clause ('(there will be) sometimes a low, sometimes a high').
segments.s551-g19: چنین است: 'it is thus' or 'such is' (a formulaic opening). کردار گردان سپهر: a nominal phrase 'the deed/behavior of the turning/revolving heaven(s)'; گردان is a participial/adjectival form modifying سپهر ('the revolving heavens'), so the whole phrase names the mutable/heavy-handed action of the sky.
segments.s551-g20: گهی ... گاه: parallel adverbial words meaning 'sometimes' (گهی) and 'at times' (گاه), framing two possible outcomes. درد پیش آردت: 'درد' (affliction) is the object; پیش + آردت = 'brings before you' (آردت = آرد + ت, 'brings you' with enclitic -ت). مهر is the other noun (favor/kindness); the verb is again omitted after گاه by ellipsis ('(it brings) favor').
segments.s551-g21: گهی بخت گردد: 'گهی' = 'sometimes'; 'بخت گردد' = 'fortune turns / becomes' (گردد = becomes/occurs, 3rd person). چو اسپی شموس: 'چو' = 'as/like'; 'اسپی' (poetic form of اسپ) 'a steed/horse' and 'شموس' (poetic genitive without ezâfe) 'of the suns' or 'of the splendid ones' — a simile describing how fortune arrives.
segments.s551-g22: به نعم اندرون زفتی آردت و بوس: به + نعم = 'with/into bounty' or 'bringing bounty'; اندرون = 'inside/inner quarters' (destination); آردت = 'brings you' (آرد + enclitic -ت); و بوس = 'and (brings) kisses' — here nouns follow the verb by ellipsis (the verb 'brings' applies to both 'نعم ... و بوس').
segments.s551-g23: زمانی یکی باره‌ای: adverbial cluster meaning 'at times / once in a while / in a single instant' (یکی + باره‌ای = 'one occasion' / 'at one stroke'). ساخته: past participle or perfect-stative 'made' or 'constituted' (here stylistically elliptical: 'one is made/raised' or 'is established').
segments.s551-g24: ز فرهختگی: ز = 'from/because of' (poetic form); فرهختگی = 'erudition / culturedness' (source or cause). سر برافراخته: 'سر' (head) + 'برافراخته' (raised/upright) — a compound nominal phrase meaning 'he raises his head' or 'his head is lifted up (in pride)'.
segments.s551-g161: This is a vocative/direct address starting with the pronoun 'تو' (you/thou) followed by the noun phrase 'پند پدر' (the father's advice). 'همچنین' ('thus/likewise') modifies the following imperative or exhortation; 'یاددار' here functions as an exhortative phrase 'keep in mind / remember' (classical compound form), so the whole segment means 'You, keep your father's counsel in mind.'
segments.s551-g162: 'به نیکی گرای' is an imperative/exhortation construction: 'به' (toward) + 'نیکی' (goodness) + 'گرای' (incline/turn toward). The following coordinate clause 'و بدی باد دار' continues the exhortation: 'و' = and; 'بدی' = evil; 'باد' is the optative/particle meaning 'may' or used in wishes/exhortations; 'دار' here is a verbal form meaning 'have/hold/keep' (classical usage: 'keep/ward off'). Together: 'incline to goodness and keep (away) evil' (i.e., shun evil).
segments.s551-g163: 'به خیره' is an adverbial phrase 'with astonishment / into amazement'; 'مرنجان' is a negative imperative 'do not vex / do not afflict' (causative sense); 'روان مرا' is the object 'my spirit/soul' with the object/topic marker 'مرا' attached after the noun. Grammatically the imperative governs this object: 'Do not afflict my soul with astonishment.'
segments.s551-g164: This is a second coordinated object of the same negative imperative: 'به آتش' (into/into the fire), 'تن ناتوان' (the powerless/weak body — noun followed by its adjective), and 'مرا' (object/topic marker) marking it as the verb's object. The previous negative command applies to both objects: 'and do not throw/expose my helpless body into the fire.'
segments.s551-g165: 'به بد کردن' uses 'به' + verbal noun 'بد کردن' ('to do bad / to make bad') and is followed by the possessive 'خویش' (your own disposition — here split into 'خوی' + enclitic 'ش'). The construction expresses an action to be avoided: 'making your nature bad.' The clause then adds 'و آزار کس' ('and the harm/annoyance of anyone') as another thing to avoid.
segments.s551-g166: 'مجوی‌ای' is a negative imperative 'do not seek' addressed vocatively: the clitic-like vocative particle '-ای' follows to call 'پسر' (son/youth). The phrase 'درد و تیمار کس' lists the objects 'pain and care of anyone' (i.e., causing or seeking to cause someone's suffering or the need for care). So: 'O son, do not seek to cause anyone pain or the need for care.'
segments.s551-g167: 'برین' is the classical compound 'بر این' (on/over this) written together; 'بگذرد' is 3rd-person singular present/future (narrative) of 'گذرادن/گذشتن' meaning 'will pass'; 'سالیان پانصد' is the time expression 'five hundred years' (noun 'سالیان' followed by the numeral 'پانصد' as is common in classical word order). So: 'Over this five hundred years will pass.'
segments.s551-g168: 'بزرگی شما را' is the noun phrase 'your greatness' with the object/topic marker 'را' (marking it as the thing affected); 'به پایان رسد' is a compound verbal phrase 'reach to the end / come to an end' where 'رسد' is 3rd-person singular of 'رسیدن' (here 'attain / reach'). Taken together it states that that greatness will attain its end (i.e., will end).
segments.s551-g129: This is a temporal subordinate clause introduced by the archaic/poetic conjunction چونک ('چون‌ک'), placed right after the subject. Word order is subject (هوا) + conjunction (چونک) + prepositional phrase (بر تخت حشمت) + finite verb (نشست) at the end — a typical Persian SOV arrangement for subordinate clauses. نشَست is a past tense verb meaning ‘sat/settled’. هِوا here is a poetic noun meaning an inner desire/caprice rather than literal ‘air’. حشمت is a possessive noun 'majesty' modified by تخت ('throne') in the phrase بر تخت حشمت = 'upon the throne of majesty.'
segments.s551-g130: نباشی is the negative present-subjunctive/imperative form (ن + باشی) addressed to the second person—here functioning as a prohibition or statement of what will not obtain. The predicate lists two coordinated adjectives/nouns خردمند و یزدان‌پرست linked by و. In context the whole clause follows the temporal clause, giving the consequence: when caprice sits, you are (or should not be) wise and God-worshipping.
segments.s551-g131: نباید here is an impersonal modal ('it is not proper/one must not') and is followed by که introducing a subordinate clause. باشی is the present-subjunctive/optative form (second person) after نباید (i.e. 'you should not be'). فراوان modifies سخن (’much’/’many’), and the object سخن precedes the verb باشی (typical Persian order: object before predicate in such constructions).
segments.s551-g132: به روی کسان is an idiomatic prepositional phrase meaning 'in the presence of/ before people' (به + روی + کسان). پارسایی مکن is a negative imperative: مکن ('do not do') with پارسایی (piety) as the object — literally 'do not make/display piety.' Word order places the prepositional phrase before the verb phrase for emphasis.
segments.s551-g133: سخن بشنو is an imperative clause where the object سخن ('speech') precedes the verb بَشنو ('listen!') — common in Persian commands. و connects a second command; بهترین یادگیر is an elliptical/adjectival phrase meaning 'become the best learner' (یادگیر is used as 'learner' and the verb 'باش' is omitted but implied), so the sequence gives two imperatives: 'listen and be the best learner.'
segments.s551-g134: نگر is an imperative 'look/consider' (here 'observe/see'). تا introduces a purposive/result clause 'so that / to see' (تا کدام آیدت...) and is followed by کدام 'which' as the interrogative/indefinite pronoun. آیدت is the present subjunctive or simple-present sense 'comes' with the clitic -ت 'to you' attached (object/dative), and دلپذیر is the predicate adjective 'pleasant' describing what may come to you. Overall: 'watch/consider so that you may see which one will please you.'
segments.s551-g135: The phrase begins with the object/subject marker سخن followed by the locative/prepositional phrase پیش فرهنگیان ('in front of / before the learned'). سخته گوی is a compound where سخته (difficult/harsh) modifies گوی (here 'speech'/'saying' or 'speaker'); together they convey 'one who speaks harshly' or 'harsh-speaking.' Word order places the adverbial location before this descriptive phrase.
segments.s551-g136: گه is an adverb 'sometimes' introducing a series of predicative descriptions. می‌نوازنده is a present-stem participle used nominally 'one who plays' (a musician) — the progressive/ habitual prefix می- plus stem, functioning as an agent noun here. و links the final attribute تازه‌روی ('fresh-faced' / 'newcomer'), another nominal/adjectival description. Together they list alternating modes one may take: 'sometimes a player, and (sometimes) a fresh-faced one.'
segments.s551-g217: چنین است is a fixed formula meaning 'such is' or 'thus is.' آیین خرم جهان is a nominal phrase: آیین (custom/practice) followed by the attributive phrase خرم جهان ('joyous world' or 'the world's joy'), so the whole segment reads 'Such is the custom of the joyous world.' Word order is predicate first (چنین است) then the predicate-noun phrase.
segments.s551-g218: نخواهد is a classical/future-like auxiliary here meaning 'will not / will not allow.' بما is the dative pronoun 'for/to us.' برگشادن is the archaic infinitive 'to turn back / to return.' نهان functions adverbially 'secretly' (placed after the infinitive). The clause literally reads 'it will not (permit) for us to turn back secretly.'
segments.s551-g219: انوشه is a proper name presented first (vocative or topical). کسی کو here means 'someone who' (کسی = someone; کو is an old/poetic relative particle meaning 'who'/'that'), followed by بزرگی ندید 'has not seen greatness' (ندید is past of دیدن). The clause describes 'Anoushe' as 'one who did not see greatness.'
segments.s551-g220: نبایستش is the classical modal construction ('it was not right for him' / 'he ought not'), with the enclitic -ش 'him/it.' از تخت indicates source/place ('from the throne'), شد is the auxiliary 'became,' and ناپدید is the predicate 'vanished' (used adjectivally/verbally). Together: 'he ought not to have become vanished from the throne' → 'he should not have disappeared from the throne.'
segments.s551-g221: بکوشی is an exhortative form (second-person imperative/subjunctive) meaning 'strive!' or 'make an effort.' و آری is a connective 'and / indeed' adding emphasis. ز هرگونه چیز is a prepositional phrase 'from every kind of thing' (ز = from; هرگونه = any kind; چیز = thing) that sets the domain for the following negative statement.
segments.s551-g222: نه ... نه ... is the correlative negative 'neither ... nor ...' with مردم and آن چیز as the two items. ماند is the verb 'remain' (here general/factual sense 'will remain' or 'endure'), and به نیز functions as an archaic/emphatic particle sequence meaning 'also / likewise.' The clause: 'neither the people nor that thing will remain either.'
segments.s551-g223: سرانجام is an adverb 'at the end / finally.' با خاک is the prepositional phrase 'with the earth' (با = with; خاک = earth/dust). باشیم is 1st person plural subjunctive/present ('we be' / 'we shall be') used here in a general/final sense, and جفت is a predicate noun 'paired/together' — 'in the end we will be paired with the earth' (i.e. return to dust).
segments.s551-g224: دو رخ را is the definite object 'the two faces' (دو = two; رخ = face; را = object marker). به چادر indicates the place/instrument 'in/under a shroud/veil.' بباید is the modal 'must / ought' and نهفت is the participial/adjectival verb 'be hidden / conceal(ed).' The combination means 'both faces must be hidden by the shroud.'
segments.s551-g1: چو introduces a temporal clause meaning 'when' (classical use). سال اندر آمد is a past-tense clause literally 'the year came in/entered' and به هفتاد و هشت specifies the quantity/age ('to seventy and eight' = 78); note Persian places the numeral after به. The coordinating و simply links 'هفتاد' and 'هشت' to form 78.
segments.s551-g2: This clause states the main result: جهاندار (literally 'world‑holder', here 'sovereign') is the subject and بیدار is an epithet 'awake/alert' modifying him. بیمار گشت is a past periphrastic: بیمار (adjective 'sick') + گشت ('became') = 'became sick.' Persian order here places modifiers before the predicate.
segments.s551-g3: بفرمود is an honorific/past verb 'he commanded/ordered' (classical register). تا here functions as a purposive/illative 'so that' or 'and so', introducing the following result clause. رفت is the simple past 'went', with the subject شاپور placed after the verb (poetic inversion). پیش means 'in the presence of' or 'before' (i.e. went to appear before him).
segments.s551-g4: ورا is the unstressed object pronoun 'to him'. پندها is the plural noun 'admonitions/advice' and داد is the past 'gave' (so 'gave him advice'). ز here is the poetic preposition equivalent to از meaning 'about/from/concerning'; اندازه بیش 'beyond measure' = 'excess' or 'too much' (اندازه = measure/degree, بیش = excess).
segments.s551-g5: بدانست is past 'he knew' or 'he perceived.' کامد is an archaic/classical present/impersonal form of 'come' meaning 'is coming' or 'will come' (here: 'was coming'). به نزدیک مرگ places 'near death' after the verb: به (toward) + نزدیک (approaching) + مرگ (death) = 'death was approaching.'
segments.s551-g6: This is a prophetic/general statement: همی is a classical adverbial particle (here an intensifier; common in older Persian). زرد خواهد شدن is a periphrastic future/passive‑like construction: زرد (yellow) + خواهد (future auxiliary 'will') + شدن ('become') = 'will become yellow.' سبز برگ (green leaf) is the patient/subject of that becoming, with the adjective preceding the noun as usual.
segments.s551-g7: بدو گفت is 'he said to him' (بدو = to him; گفت = said). کاین is the fused demonstrative/relative 'that this' or 'that which is' introducing direct speech. عهد من 'my covenant/pledge' has the possessor after the noun (عهد = pledge, من = my/I). یاددار here functions as a lexicalized admonition 'keep in memory' or 'let it be remembered' (classical usage where یاد = memory and دار = keep).
segments.s551-g8: همه here addresses 'all' or 'let everyone' as a general injunction; گفت repeats the speechive idea 'he said' or 'he commanded' that introduces the following clause. بدگوی ('ill‑speaker' or 'slanderer') is the person marked by را as definite object; باددار is a classical optative/imperative formula (باد + دار) meaning 'may it be kept/guarded' or 'keep away' in context — essentially an imperative formula asking that the slanderer be guarded against or kept off.
segments.s551-g73: هرانگه که introduces a temporal/conditional clause ('whenever/when'), with که as the subordinating particle. خشم آورد is an idiomatic past action: آورد (here causative/bring) with خشم expresses 'feel/become angry' (the verb آورد follows the noun it affects). پادشا is the subject 'the king' placed after the verb phrase in this poetic word order.
segments.s551-g74: سبک‌مایه خواند is a past-tense clause where خواند (here = 'call/name') takes سبک‌مایه as the name/epithet given. ورا ('to him/it') is the object/adverbial pointing to the person so named, and پارسا is the recipient noun 'the pious one' — the phrase means 'he called the pious one light‑minded.' Word order (name + verb + object) is compressed and typical of poetic Persian.
segments.s551-g75: چو introduces a general circumstance ('when/since/if'); بر شاه marks the target ('toward the king') with بر before the direct object. زشتست is a predicative adjective 'is unbecoming/wrong' describing the infinitival/substantive phrase بد خواستن ('wishing ill'); خواستن here functions as the verbal noun 'to wish' so the whole clause means 'it is unbecoming to wish bad toward the king.'
segments.s551-g76: بباید is the modal 'must/ought' and governs the following infinitival idea. به خوبی is an adverbial phrase 'with goodness / in goodness' (به + noun used adverbially), دل آراستن is an infinitive/verbal phrase 'to adorn the heart' (آراستن as the action, دل the object). The structure states obligation followed by the purpose/content to be fulfilled.
segments.s551-g77: وگر is the conditional 'if / but if'. بیم داری is present-tense 'you have fear' (داری = 'you have'). به دل is the locative expression 'in the heart' (به here meaning 'in/into/toward'), and یک زمان is a temporal phrase 'for a moment / a time.' The clause literally reads 'if you have fear in the heart for a moment.'
segments.s551-g78: شود is the impersonal/predictive 'becomes / will become.' خیره رای is a compound where خیره ('astonished/dazed') qualifies رای ('opinion / counsel / decision'), so 'counsel becomes bewildered.' از + بد بدگمان gives the cause: از 'because of / from' + بد (qualifying word) + بدگمان 'ill suspicion / distrust' — literally 'becomes dazed in counsel from bad suspicion.'
segments.s551-g79: This line is elliptical/poetic. ز is the poetic preposition 'from/of.' بخشش is the noun 'forgiveness/grant.' منه here is irregular in the given dictionary senses (none fit cleanly) and functions in the poetic clause as an imperative/elliptical expression meaning roughly 'do not deny/withhold' or 'do not be without' — the phrase as a whole commands not to remove forgiveness. بر دل اندوه نیز places 'sorrow upon the heart also' (بر + دل = 'on the heart', اندوه = 'sorrow', نیز = 'also').
segments.s551-g80: بدان is the imperative 'know' (here formed by the prefix ب- + دان). تا توان is a limit clause 'as far as you can' or 'to the extent of your ability' (تا meaning 'as far as / to the extent that', توان = 'can/ability'). ای پسر is a vocative 'O son.' ارج چیز is a noun phrase 'the value/honor of a thing' (ارج preceding چیز in poetic order), completing the admonition: 'Know, O son, the worth of a thing as much as you can.'
segments.s551-g41: چو is the conjunction 'when/if' introducing a conditional/time clause. باشد is the poetic/subjunctive/copiular form 'is/should be' (3rd-person), here functioning as the verb of the clause. خداوند رای و خرد is a noun phrase 'the Lord's counsel and wisdom' (خداوند as the possessor + رای و خرد listing two abstract qualities). Word order is the older/poetic order where the predicate (رای و خرد) follows the possessor.
segments.s551-g42: دو گیتی is a fixed poetic expression 'the two worlds' (i.e. heaven and earth). همی is the archaic/perfective-present particle marking ongoing or general action (it often accompanies verbs in classical Persian). مرد دینی is the subject 'a pious man' (مرد + adjectival دینی). برد here is used as a verbal idea 'gains / takes' (poetic usage meaning 'obtains, wins').
segments.s551-g43: چو begins a conditional 'if/when'. دین را uses the accusative/definite marker را on 'religion' (here the thing guarded). بود is the copula 'was/is' (classical form), and پادشا پاسبان is the predicate 'the king (is) the guardian/protector' (predicate nouns follow the copula in this poetic order).
segments.s551-g44: تو is the vocative/subject 'you' (poetic 'thou'). این هر دو را is the object phrase 'these two' with را marking it as definite/accusative. جز introduces the exclusion 'except/other than' but here combines with the negative imperative that follows to mean 'do not call ... a brother.' برادر is the noun 'brother,' and مخوان is a negative imperative (from خواندن) meaning 'do not call/name/consider.' The whole clause is an address: 'you, do not call either of these two a brother.'
segments.s551-g45: چو introduces the conditional 'if/when.' دین‌دار is a compound 'one who holds/has religion' = 'a pious/devout person' (subject). کین دارد is the verb phrase 'has hatred / harbors enmity' (کین = grudge/enmity, دارد = 'has'). از پادشا marks the target of that hostility ('toward the king') using از in the classical sense 'from/against' with a meaning close to 'against.'
segments.s551-g46: مخوان is the negative imperative 'do not call/consider' (again from خواندن used in the sense 'to name/consider someone X'). تا توانی is an adverbial phrase 'as far as you can / to the extent of your ability' (تا + توانى). ورا is the enclitic object 'him/to him,' and پارسا is the predicate adjective 'pious.' The structure says: 'do not call him pious, as much as you can.'
segments.s551-g47: هرانکس که is the indefinite relative 'whoever / whosoever who.' بر here functions as 'against' (classical poetic usage), so بر دادگر شهریار forms the prepositional phrase 'against the just king' (دادگر = 'just, righteous', شهریار = 'king'). This clause sets up the person targeted by the following command.
